question_text: Which of the following correctly describes the relationship between power and energy?

options.label: A
options.text: Power is the total amount of energy used by a device

options.label: B
options.text: Power is the rate at which energy is transferred or used

options.label: C
options.text: Power and energy are measured in the same units

options.label: D
options.text: Power increases whenever energy is stored

correct_answer: Power is the rate at which energy is transferred or used
explanation: Power tells us how quickly energy is being used or transferred. It's measured in watts (W), which is the same as joules per second (J/s). This is different from energy, which measures the total amount and is measured in joules (J). A more powerful device uses or transfers energy faster than a less powerful one.
